title: AN ACT SEPARATING THE CAGASAT NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL â MAGSAYSAY ANNEX IN BARANGAY MAGSAYSAY, MUNICIPALITY OF CORDON, PROVINCE OF ISABELA FROM THE CAGASAT NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL, CONVERTING IT INTO AN INDEPENDENT NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL TO BE KNOWN AS CORDON NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL AND APPROPRIATION FUNDS THEREFOR

chunks.text:
H. No. 2817 ; Manila Bulletin (August 1, 2016); 112 OG No. 33, 5196 (August 15, 2016)

[ REPUBLIC ACT NO. 10872, July 17, 2016 ]

AN ACT SEPARATING THE CAGASAT NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL â MAGSAYSAY ANNEX IN BARANGAY MAGSAYSAY, MUNICIPALITY OF CORDON, PROVINCE OF ISABELA FROM THE CAGASAT NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL, CONVERTING IT INTO AN INDEPENDENT NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL TO BE KNOWN AS CORDON NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL AND APPROPRIATION FUNDS THEREFOR

Be it enacted by the Senate and House of Representatives of the Philippines in Congress assembled:

SECTION 1.

Separation and Conversion into a National High School

â The Cagasat National High School â Magsaysay Annex in Barangay Magsaysay, Municipality of Cordon, Province of Isabela is hereby separated from the Cagasat National High School and converted into an independent national high school to be known as cordon National High School.

SEC. 2.

Transfer of Assets and Liabilities.

â All personnel, assets, liabilities and records of the Cagasat National High School â Magsaysay Annex are hereby transferred to and absorbed by the Cordon National High School.

SEC. 3.

Appropriation

â The Secretary of Education shall immediately include in the Department's program the operationalization of the Cordon National High School, the initial funding of which shall be charged against the current yearâs appropriation of the Cagasat National High School â Magsaysay Annex. Thereafter, the amount necessary for the continued operation of the school shall be included in the annual General Appropriations Act.

SEC. 4.

Implementation

â The Secretary of Education shall issue rules and regulations necessary to carry out the purpose of this Act.

SEC. 4.

Implementation

â The Secretary of Education shall issue rules and regulations necessary to carry out the purpose of this Act.

SEC. 5.

Effectivity

â This Act shall take effect fifteen (15) days after its publication in the

Official Gazette

or in a newspaper of general circulation.

Approved,

(Sgd.)

FRANKLIN M. DRILON

President of the Senate

(Sgd.)

FELICIANO BELMONTE JR.

Speaker of the House

of Representatives

This Act which originated in the House of Representatives was passed by the House of Representatives and the Senate of February 23, 2016 and May 23, 2016, respectively.

(Sgd.)

OSCAR G. YABES

Secretary of the Senate

(Sgd.)

MARILYN B. BARUA-YAP

Secretary General

House of Representatives

Approved:

BENIGNO S. AQUINO III

President of the Philippines

Lapsed into law on

JUL 17 2016

Without the signature of the President

In accordance with Article VI, Section

27 (1) of the Constitution.
